blocks.content: After spending a day at the mansion, Shirone headed to the dining room with Rian and Tess.
Clump was seated at the head of the table, reading a newspaper, while Reina was busy preparing breakfast.
Shirone rubbed his sleepy eyes and greeted them with a bow.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Good morning.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Morning. Let's eat.
Rian, rubbing his hungry stomach, sat down and asked Reina,

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Where's Rai?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: He left at dawn. Do you think he's as idle as you?
As usual, she started with a joke, but this time there was a hint of concern.
As an older sister, she couldn't help but worry about her younger brother leaving on a knight's quest.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hey, I only dropped out yesterday! And I'm leaving right after breakfast.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Why don't you rest for a few days? Shirone is here too.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I need to head back soon as well.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He couldn't explain the details due to his promise with Kangnan.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Moreover, he was still curious why the chairman wanted to see him.
Since it was the day Rian was leaving, the atmosphere was quite solemn.
However, Rian himself devoured three plates of chicken stew and hurriedly packed his things.
His belongings were simple: a backpack with some clothes and a day's worth of food.
With a leather scabbard strapped to his body and the greatsword on his back, he approached Clump in the living room.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Grandfather.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Yes, are you ready?
There was one thing left to address.
The fact that he had defeated Rai would give him great courage for his journey.
But there was something he needed to clarify.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What did Rai mean yesterday?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Why didn't he use swordsmanship? You know, don't you?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Oh, that.
Clump smacked his lips and put down his teacup.
He had been putting it off to avoid giving Rian false hopes, but now that Rian was leaving on a knight's quest, it was time to tell him.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: The sword you carry is not an ordinary one. Have you heard of an object?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: An object?
Rian's eyes widened as he drew the greatsword.
The functions of precise control and Vajra Armor that Shirone had demonstrated the previous night flashed through his mind, making his heart race.
Shirone and Tess also came into the living room, intrigued by the conversation, and focused on the greatsword.
It was a sword with no special features other than its imposing design.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: So this is an object? Is that why it wasn't destroyed? What abilities does it have?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: The official name of that sword is . Its abilities are as impressive as its name.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: To put it bluntly, it cannot be destroyed.
Silence filled the living room.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: And?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: What do you mean, 'and'? It can't be destroyed. Whether it falls into lava, gets cut with a saw, or hit with a hammer, it won't break.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Isn't that amazing?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: In a way, it was a swordsman's dream.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: But it's still an object. If a sword breaks, can't you just buy a new one?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Foolish. Do you know how important a weapon's durability is in real combat?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Even in yesterday's duel, it would have been much harder without .
Rian acknowledged this.
In fact, he had already been saved by once.
It was when he blocked Ymir's straight punch.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Ymir had crumpled the steel-made Signa and Exde like paper, but he couldn't destroy my greatsword.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: If not for , Ymir's punch would have gone through the sword and shattered my body.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: That's the kind of sword it is.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Rai didn't avoid using swordsmanship; he couldn't.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: When he was young, he really wanted that sword.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Then why didn't you give it to Rai?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: He doesn't like crude things. It wouldn't suit him.
The answer was different from what Rian had expected.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Who said that? Stop pretending to read minds.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Heh heh, what did you expect? That I prepared it especially for you?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: You made me nervous for nothing. I thought you had some life-and-death experience.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Hahaha! Do you think I'm crazy to risk my life for a sword?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: By the way, how much did you pay? Since it's an object, maybe around 2 million gold?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: That? 80 million gold.
Clang! Rian dropped the greatsword.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Damn! Are you crazy? You paid 80 million for a sword that just doesn't break? You could buy hundreds of fine swords with that money!

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: It's an object, you fool! And it's my money, so why do you care?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: If you're going to treat it like that, give it back! Or pay me 80 million!

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: No way! You gave it to me, so that's it. No take-backs!
Rian hurriedly picked up the greatsword and hugged it, afraid someone might take it.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: What kind of swordsman throws his sword on the ground? That sword is too good for you!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hang it in my room!

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Why? Afraid it might break? Didn't you say a true sword is held by the heart?
Shirone and Tess watched the argument in disbelief.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They're so alike.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Indeed.
After arguing for a while, they finally stopped, exhausted.
In the end, Rian won.
He sheathed the greatsword and tied the leather strap tighter than usual, as if to show Clump.
After a moment of huffing, Clump smiled, remembering something.
It was a moment he had waited 18 years for.
Seeing it with his own eyes made the old man's heart swell with emotion.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: The more I see him, the more he resembles me.
He had little talent for swordsmanship, but Rian was always meant to be the owner of .
He hadn't paid 80 million gold for its functions.
An indestructible sword was the perfect symbol of the Ozent family's conviction.
And in this moment, all his philosophy was passed to Rian through .

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: It's strange. When I went back after paying 80 million gold, the village had vanished.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Of course, if 80 million gold had come to a mountain village, the entire village could have moved to a better place.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: However, there was a subtle feeling Clump couldn't share with Rian.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Perhaps he had only been lying injured in the mountains, and the month he spent going back and forth between the village and the house was actually a dream he wandered through in a state of unconsciousness.
In any case, it was one of the most inexplicable events in Clump's life.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Grandfather, I'm off now.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Yes. The most important thing is to protect yourself.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Remember that what you gain from real combat isn't always victory.
It was advice to avoid fights when possible.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Taking your grandfather's advice to heart, Rian bowed sincerely.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Thank you, Grandfather, for believing in your foolish grandson until the end.
Rian left the mansion, accompanied by his family's and friends' farewells.
A carriage was waiting at the front gate.
After briefly contemplating his destination, Rian made up his mind and turned to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shirone, do your best. Even though I dropped out, just think of it as me graduating first.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sure. Take care of yourself. Keep in touch.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Tess, I'm off.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: You jerk. You didn't even discuss it with me...

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Sorry. I didn't think it through.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: This is the last time I'll tolerate it. If you treat me this coldly when you return...

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I'll come back stronger.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Alright. Come back stronger.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: You don't have to become stronger. Just come back alive...
Having said his goodbyes to everyone, Rian got into the carriage.
True to his nature, he didn't look back once.
As the carriage departed, the four left behind felt an emptiness.
That was Rian's presence.
Shirone watched the receding carriage and prayed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Come back as a great swordsman, Rian. I'll do my best too.
May good fortune be with Rian on his journey.
